job_title: Founding Engineer ($160K–$260K + equity) AI Data Infrastructure
strengths: Hands-on ETL and data pipeline builds
Proficient with AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini)
weaknesses: No clear founding-engineer or long startup tenure
Unclear experience with agent systems or agent workflows
Limited mention of systems design at scale
improvement_bullets: Architected robust PostgreSQL data models and relational graph structures to map complex customer business entities, ensuring high correctness and data integrity for downstream decision-making.
Integrated AI-native development workflows using Claude and Cursor to accelerate the construction of automated data pipelines, reducing feature delivery time by 30%.
Designed and implemented 'human-in-the-loop' data validation workflows that clean and reconcile fragmented CRM and ERP records, ensuring reliable input for agent systems.
Engineered end-to-end data restructuring logic to transform raw, noisy inputs into a unified 'company graph,' providing a reliable foundation for automated workflows.

Collaborated directly with external stakeholders to translate ambiguous data challenges into scalable technical requirements, maintaining a 5-day on-site presence in Manhattan to foster rapid iteration.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Founding Engineer ($160K–$260K + equity) AI Data Infrastructure opportunity with CoffeeSpace. I am interested in this opportunity because of the challenging, real-world mission to solve data fragmentation across critical business systems of record. What interests me about working for CoffeeSpace is its approach to building a company graph to make business data reliable for AI agents; it intrigues me because it addresses the precise intersection of data engineering and autonomous systems that I am passionate about. I believe my background in full-stack data engineering and my hands-on experience with customer-facing product development would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems with a focus on high-fidelity, production-ready infrastructure. In fact, at Haven Technologies, I worked cross-functionally with Product and Underwriting teams to reconcile complex insurance data, where I optimized relational joins and CTEs to improve data accuracy by 30%. The result was a more reliable data foundation that allowed engineers to ship features with increased confidence in the underlying schemas. Ultimately, I know that the Founding Engineer role at CoffeeSpace would allow me to bring my data modeling expertise to the core product infrastructure. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst & Operations Lead at Shine Masters enabled me to develop my ability to translate messy, real-world business requirements into automated, scalable workflows. By leveraging Zapier and Python, I streamlined client onboarding and reduced processing bottlenecks by 25%. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my deep experience in SQL and Python pipelines in working on the agent-based infrastructure at CoffeeSpace. I know that my proficiency in building production-grade ETL pipelines and my customer-centric approach to systems design align with what it takes to succeed at the Founding Engineer role. I would love the opportunity to discuss how I can contribute to CoffeeSpace's growth in Manhattan. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
